name: Velibor Bojkovic
title: Visiting Assistant Professor of Machine Learning

tabs.biography:
Prior to joining MBZUAI as faculty, Professor Bojkovic worked as a researcher at MBZUAI, first as a Research Fellow working on a project related to Spiking Neural Networks and then as a Research Scientist working on a project related to applications of LLMs in the energy sector. Before that, he worked as a researcher in various areas of pure mathematics.


At the University of Normandie in Caen (France), he was a member of Number Theory group where he worked on non-archimedean spaces, ramification theory of local fields and theory of Drinfeld modules. Before that at the University of Padova (Italy), Professor Bojkovic was member of p-adic geometry group working on p-adic differential equations and ramification theory of finite morphisms of non-archimedean curves.


In addition, he worked at the Einstein Institute of Mathematics at Haifa University (Israel) in the area of non-Archimedean spaces. He has a double Ph.D. degree in theoretical mathematics from the University of Padova and the University of Bordeaux.
tabs.education: Research Scientist and Research Fellow, Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence (MBZUAI), UAE Postdoctoral position, University of Caen Normandie, France Postdoctoral position, University of Padova, Italy Postdoctoral position, Einstein Institute of Mathematics at HAIFA, Israel Ph.D. in Theoretical Mathematics, University of Padova (Italy) / University of Bordeaux (France) M.Sc. in Mathematics, University of Bordeaux (France) /University of Padova (Italy) B.Sc. in Theoretical Mathematics, Faculty of Mathematics and Natural Sciences, Montenegro
